title: How one ancestor came to America and a family chest and its westward trek
description: Ancestor kidnapped on a ship and worked his way from Ireland to America. Story of an ancestral chest and its westward trek.

title: Meeting an Indian in winter. Grandmother braves Indians to get her rug striped.
description: Meeting an Indian in winter. Grandmother braves Indians to get her rug striped.

title: Why she moved to Troy; purchasing the newspaper
description: Moving to Troy to work halftime on the Troy Weekly News with Mr. Jones for whom she'd been correspondent in Moore, Montana. She purchased the newspaper.

title: Why she sold the newspaper; a man complained because his relative was given credit as a businesswoman
description: She sold out because of opposition among some highly placed foreign-born men to a woman owning a business. English tradition of female subservience. A man complained because his relative was given credit as a businesswoman.

title: Father was a Republican; hear a speech given by a suffragette in South Dakota
description: Father a staunch Republican. An orator explained "free silver." A speech by a suffragette about women's right to vote in Redfield, South Dakota.

title: Living in a women's dormitory build by a women's group at Redfield College
description: A women's group got a dormitory for women built at Redfield College. A poor woman student who managed to get through school there.

title: Women's Christian Temperance Union is family tradition; Grandparents' courtship and marriage; how grandparents got started in WCTU
description: WCTU as part of the family tradition. The old-time pioneer women did not talk freely. Grandparents' courtship and marriage; their conversion by an evangelist to the WCTU.

title: Grandmother's gift for gardening; experiment of growing food form a single potato
description: Grandmother's gift for gardening. Experiment of growing a great deal of food from a single potato, (cont.)

title: working as a jail visitor for WCTU; discussions of different people she saw as a jail visitor
description: Working as "jail visitor" for the WCTU. Story of a boy set-up by a gang. "Jail visitor" as a department of WCTU, carried on individually. A mentally disturbed boy.

title: WCTU doesn't do much except fight booze; trying to locate needy families for Thanksgiving dinner
description: WCTU does much work besides "booze fighting" Difficulty locating needy family to give Thanksgiving dinner.

title: Stories of how she gave charity in the early days
description: Giving charity in early days - a painter who drank his earnings; a lady who wouldn't take a dinner. Visiting a terrible home; they were forced to leave town.

title: Mother fed all hungry men who came to the door
description: Suffering of people in hard times. Mother fed all hungry men who came to her door. A businessman begged Mrs. Grove for food in DesMoines. (cont.)

title: More about hard times. A tramp and a businessman traveling together.
description: More about hard times. A tramp and a businessman traveling together.

title: A mysterious woman who appeared on Mrs. Grove's doorstep in Moscow.
description: A mysterious tramp who appeared on Mrs. Grove's doorstep in Moscow.

title: Acquiring and living in her Moscow home. Paying a carpenter in lean years.
description: Acquiring and living in her Moscow home. Paying a carpenter in lean years.

title: Boarding college boys in Moscow; advantages of having butcher choose meat cuts
description: Boarding college boys at her home (1932-1934); one is still in touch with her. Two big-eating freshmen who fortunately went elsewhere. Congeniality and appreciation of the boys. Their sadness at the close of the year. Advantage of having the butcher choose the meat.

title: She matriculated at the university at the age of eighty-two.
description: She matriculated at the university at the age of eighty-two.
